observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare the necessary journal entries to record the reconciling items in the books;
Cause the reconciliation of the total amounts of CIB-LCCA between subsidiary ledgers and general ledger under the General Fund; and,
Maintain subsidiary ledgers on all bank accounts maintained by the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the completion of the project and the transfer of its cost from the Trust Fund to the General Fund, and ensure that such and all other financial transactions are recorded in accordance with the pertinent accounting guidelines to present fairly the Statements of financial position, financial performance, and cash flows of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to adjust the identified misstatement and review and reconcile the deficiencies noted to come up with a reliable balance of Due to BIR account as at year end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to review and reconcile the variances in order to arrive at the correct amount of premium contributions to GSIS and be remitted accordingly to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries in the Trust Fund books to correct the identified misstatements of the related expense accounts in the Financial Statements. Henceforth, observe the proper recording of like expense in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries in the books to correct the identified misstatements of the related accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to ensure that all development projects to be funded under 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the current year unexpended QRF and MF- MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under Trust Liabilities-DRRM account; and
The LDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the balance of prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF with updated programs or activities, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other members of the GAD Focal Point System ensure the implementation of GAD-related PPAs through proper gender mainstreaming or attribution of GAD fund to the programs, projects and activities of the LGU, to fully address the gender sensitive issues in the locality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure that all cash advances, except for petty cash, should be fully liquidated at the end of each year, and observe strict adherence to the rules governing the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; and,
Send letters to employees to demand the immediate liquidation or refund of their outstanding cash advances.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC officials concerned to ensure that the proper procurement procedures and processes prescribed in RA 9184 are strictly complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
The Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements thru e-Payments are based on a duly approved DVs/Payrolls; and
The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit Daily Report of e-Payments from the Agency Account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Require the Inventory Committee to prepare a systematic and well-planned physical inventory taking, which could be done in a year-round basis, and to prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and ensure that all necessary data are provided thereon; and, b) Direct the Property and Accounting Unit to undertake collaborative procedure to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly taken up in their respective records and that the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the PPELCs maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled.
observations.managementAction:
There was yet no regular updating of supplies ledger/stock card. Subsidiary ledgers as prescribed by Section 111 (1) of PD 1445 were not maintained at time of audit.
Some necessary actions were being undertaken by the Municipal Accountant and newly appointed Property/Supply Officer.
observations.reason: Not all properties of the LGU were accounted and the necessary data such as property no. and remarks whether serviceable or unserviceable were not provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a) The LDRRM Officer, Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to regularly coordinate and reconcile their records in order to appropriately guide the LCE in monitoring the utilization and status of the fund; b) The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to make the necessary adjustments in their utilization reports and maintain the required Subsidiary Ledgers; c) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under Trust Liabilities-DRRM account at yearend and make the necessary adjusting entries; d) The Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF; and e) The LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2022 report from Accounting and Budget Office on the LDRRM Fund do not reconcile.
Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF was not submitted.
observations.reason: Unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE was not transferred. Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) To ensure that goods be procured based on functionality and performance requirements, and not on brand names; and b) To prepare BAC Resolution to duly authorize the delegation of authority to the requesting unit/end-user.
observations.managementAction:
Procurement of goods are not based on brand names.
RAO for Capital Outlay was not submitted upon our demand.
No BAC Resolution was prepared for the said purpose.
observations.reason: No BAC Resolution was prepared

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) Establish internal control procedures on the processing of payroll, in coordination with other concerned officials; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payrolls be duly supported with the required DTR, application for leave, travel orders and other required documents; and c) Direct department office heads to prepare and submit the Daily Time Records, Leave Applications, Travel Orders and Certificates of Appearance and other pertinent documents.
observations.managementAction: Payment for the monthly salaries and wages were not paid complete with the required supporting documents such as DTRs and approved application for leave. The Municipal Accountant and HRMO committed to set a deadline on the submission of DTRs.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were not submitted and payrolls lacked required attachments at time of audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs and projects in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were provided.
observations.managementAction: The GAD focal person implemented several GAD related PPAs and ensured that only PPAs which will be implemented shall be included in the GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.reason: Several GAD-related PPAs were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials or persons to settle their Notices of Suspensions and Notices of Disallowances within the prescribed period and the Municipal Accountant to send copy of the Notices to Persons Liable for their information on their unsettled suspensions/disallowance.
observations.managementAction: On-going settlement of NS/ND.
observations.reason: Some NS/ND are still outstanding

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the lacking disbursement vouchers, payrolls and liquidation reports with supporting documents and the Municipal Treasurer to refund amounts upon verification.
observations.managementAction: There was ongoing verification of cash advances and liquidation of the MT. Some documents were still being verified.
observations.reason: Partial settlement only

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the implementation of the audit recommendations towards improved financial operations and good governance.
observations.managementAction: Some recommendations implemented. Several prior recommendations were not yet complied with.
observations.reason: Many audit recommendations remain unimplemented

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:
a.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to Accounting Office a
Schedule of Unreleased Commercial Checks at the end of the year;
b.) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank
Reconciliation Statements and Bank Statement of Bank Account No.
1191-1320-05; and prepare adjusting entries to record all book reconciling items.

2.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and Supply/Property Officer or Municipal Treasurer to:
a) Investigate and reconcile immediately the discrepancies between the PPE
balances per accounting records and RPCPPE of the Inventory Committee; and,
b) Undertake collaborative procedure to ensure that all PPEs included in the
RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records.

3.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to make necessary adjustments in the General Fund books to correct the
misstatements affecting the Road Networks and Land accounts.

4.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a) The LDRRM Officer, Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to
regularly coordinate and reconcile their records in order to appropriately
guide the LCE in monitoring the utilization and status of the fund;
b) The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to make the necessary
adjustments in their utilization reports and maintain the required
Subsidiary Ledgers to accurately reflect the unexpended balance of the
LDRRM Funds; and
c) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the
unexpended QRF and DRRMF- MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under
Trust Liabilities-DRRM account at yearend and make the necessary
adjusting entries.

5.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDO and Municipal
Engineer the following courses of action:
a) Ensure that only eligible expenditures are charged against the 20% DF as
provided in the DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1
dated November 4, 2020;
b) Require the contractor to rectify the deficiencies observed on the
Construction of Multi-Purpose Building (Food Court), otherwise, a refund
of the amount corresponding to the unperformed work should be
demanded; and
c) Strictly monitor the said on-going projects to ensure the contractor’s
compliance on the terms and conditions stipulated in the contract and to
determine if the latter is already in default for possible Liquidated
Damages.

6.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning
and Development Officer and Municipal Budget Officer the following courses
of action:
a) Appropriate the 20% DF to finance the development projects embodied in
the LDP and AIP of the Municipality; and
b) Ensure that projects in the AIP must be based on the duly approved LDP
and include those PPAs which are to be implemented on that specific year.

7.14. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and Budget Officer to the following course of actions:
a) Ensure that projects implemented are well-planned so that the benefits can
be enjoyed by the constituents;
b) Finance projects which can be completed or used in 1 - 2 years; and
c) Discontinue financing various projects for different phases with an
unusable constructed portion.

8.11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to
do the following:
a) Require the contractors to rectify the deficiencies observed on the
Construction/Installation of Street Lights, Construction of Pathway in
Brgy. Cabacungan and Rehabilitation of Senior Citizen Building,
otherwise, a refund of the amount corresponding to the unperformed work
should be demanded;
b) Direct the Municipal Engineer and MPDO to closely monitor the said on-
going projects to ensure the contractor’s compliance on the terms and
conditions stipulated in the contract and to determine if the latter is
already in default for possible Liquidated Damages;

9.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
the following courses of action:
a) Require the RCCs to remit all of their collection to the treasurer/cashier
at the close of the business day;
b) Deposit all collections daily and intact or not later than the next banking
day; and
c) Discontinue the practice of partial deposit of collections.

10.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LGU personnel
concerned with the following courses of action:
a) The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to set up the PCF at the
beginning of the year under the imprest system and refrain from
processing payment and recording transactions if the supporting
documents are inadequately accomplished or not attached;
b) The Municipal Treasurer/Petty Cash Custodian to submit the supporting
documents upon replenishment of PCF and submit the liquidation
documents within 20 days after the end of the year; and
c) The Petty Cash Custodian to replenish when the disbursements have
already reached to at least 75% of the initial cash advance for the
continuity and availability of the fund at any given time of the year.

11.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
do the following courses of actions:
a) Require the RCCs to prepare and submit RAAF at least once a month;
b) Prepare and submit CRAAF monthly attached therewith the RAAF of
each RCCs for that month; and
c) Instruct the former RCC to prepare RAAF to properly turn over the
accountable forms in her custody. Henceforth, require all outgoing
collecting officers to turn over properly the accountable forms in their
custody, duly supported with RAAF.

12.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to issue demand letters and enforce the full liquidation of cash advances by
officers and employees. Henceforth, require all officials and employees to
observe strict adherence to the rules governing the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances.

13.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake the following courses
of actions:
a) Refrain from realigning or utilizing the 30% QRF for pre-disaster PPAs
or for any other purpose;
b) Order the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement
transactions from the DRRMF should be duly supported with complete
and proper documentation, before its approval for payment;
c) Direct the LDRRMO to ensure that the appropriated funds for LDRRM
shall be used solely to support disaster risk management activities; and,
d) Require the Municipal Accountant and LDRRM Officer to prepare and
submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month.

14.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should ensure that seminars
and conventions attended is responsive to the organizational result and
development outcome, keeping in mind the prudent use of the scarce financial
resources of the LGU. Also, before approving the authority to attend any
seminar-workshop, the authority and competencies of the sponsoring
organization/agency should be accordingly verified.

15.6. We recommended for the LCE the following courses of action:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant and HRMO to ensure that all claims of
monetization of vacation leave credits by the officials and employees of the
LGU are supported with the required documents pursuant to the Omnibus
Rules on Leave and COA Circular No. 2012-001;
b) Direct the HRMO that only qualified personnel of the LGU shall be
allowed to monetize a maximum of 30 days’ vacation leave/service credits,
except those allowed up to 50 percent of the total accrued leave pursuant
to Section 23 of the same Rules on Leave;
c) Instruct the HRMO to observe the limitation that five days’ vacation
leave/service credit shall be retained after monetization;
d) Oblige the HRMO to ensure that all monetization is posted and deducted
in the leave cards of the concerned personnel; and
e) Require the HRMO to limit the grant of monetization only to personnel
who have sufficient vacation leave credits.

16.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman and
BAC Secretary to submit copy of approved contracts within five (5) days from
its execution as provided for in COA Circular No. 2009-001.

17.15. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Human Resource and Management officer to assess its existing
Organizational Structure so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel
will be established and submit a report with recommendations thereon to
the LCE for approval by the appropriate bodies;
b) Instruct the HRMO to create policies and guidelines to address the issue
on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs taking into consideration the
existing regular employees;
c) Direct the HRMO to limit the hiring of personnel under Job Order
contracts to those extremely necessary and deploy only to offices that are
actually in need of their services;
d) Oblige Job Orders to submit primary personal identification, undergo
interview and evaluate the performance of each to give assurance that the
risk identified above are addressed; and,
e) Require the HRMO to incorporate in the contract the necessary details
such as the job description/specific work to be done and the hours to be
rendered, the certification as to the necessity of hiring the Job Order,
certification as to the existence of the appropriation, and that the person
duly signified the intent which was considered by the hiring agency.

18.7. We recommended for the LCE the following courses of action:
a) provide a biometric machine for the LGUs employees to register accurately
the actual office/work hours rendered and require the submission of the
DTRs based on the biometric machine entries/records;
b) require each head of office to monitor the attendance/absences of the
personnel under his supervision; and
c) require the HRMO to review or scrutinize the submitted DTR and check
if the time of log-in and log-out of office of an employee is based on the
logbook and biometric machine print out to ensure correctness of the
entries in the DTR.

19.8. We recommended for the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:
a) Create an Oversight Committee to : (1) review the current fees and charges
imposed by the LGU; (2) compute the appropriation rates for each services
rendered by the LGU, and (3) submit to the Municipal Mayor the proposed
revisions of the rates and charges;
b) Require the appointed Chairperson of the Oversight Committee, to
recommend to the SB the immediate approval of the proposed revisions of
the LGU’s Municipal Revenue Code; and,
c) Require the Municipal Vice-mayor, as the Presiding Officer of the SB to
facilitate the immediate issuance of a local revenue ordinance for the
adoption of the revised rates on fees and charges of the LGU.

20.11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the HMRO to expedite the
hiring process for the position of Internal Audit Services/Unit in the
organizational structure of the Municipality of Allen and hire
qualified/competent personnel therein to perform the functions required by the
said office.

21.12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other members of the GAD
Focal Point System ensure the implementation of GAD-related PPAs through
proper gender mainstreaming or attribution of GAD fund to the programs,
projects and activities of the LGU, to fully address the gender sensitive issues in
the locality.

22.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to render their accounts and submit the required
financial transactions and reports within the reglementary period. Henceforth,
they should be reminded to strictly observe Section 347 of RA 7160 and COA
Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of financial accounts.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:
observations.managementAction:
The stale checks were cancelled.
The dormant bank account was included in the CY 2022 FS.
The lacking vouchers were not submitted.
observations.reason: The status of dormant bank account was not verified resulting to the non-submission of BRS and Bank Statement of the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments in the books to reclassify the cost of completed projects recorded under Construction in progress accounts.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries to reclassify the amount from the respective CIP accounts to the appropriate asset accounts were accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. Stop providing allowance for depreciation on assets which has already reached its maximum useful life. b. Make necessary adjustments in the books to correct the errors.
observations.managementAction:
Depreciation on fully depreciated assets were discontinued.
Adjusting entries were prepared to correct the misstatements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) The Department Heads with unliquidated cash advance for PCF to return the unused PCF
observations.managementAction: b) The Municipal Accountant to set up at the beginning of the year the PCF under the imprest system and refrain from processing payment if the supporting documents are inadequately accomplished or not attached
observations.reason:
P CF of every department heads still existed.
PCF was not set up at the beginning of the year. Further, payments out of the PCF were not supported with the required supporting documents.
There were ineligible expenditures that were charged from the PCF in CY 2022.
The Municipal Treasurer stated she would rather discontinue the Petty Cash Fund than processing payments for various expenses without complete supporting documents. However, the Municipal Mayor opposed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to: a) Prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SEF tax receivable in the succeeding year; b) Ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the addresses; and c) Refund the excess amounts of fund transfers from various source agencies.
observations.managementAction:
MOA of the on-going construction of Super Health Center funded by DOH was not submitted. Excess fund transfers from DILG, DOTr, PENRO and BFAR were refunded.
The Municipal Accountant submitted the required supporting schedules for funded projects for all funds.
observations.reason: Excess fund from DILG fund transfer was not yet refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to: a) Ensure that only actual project expenses shall be reported in the utilization and liquidation of Fund Transfer from any source agencies
observations.managementAction: b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a revised Fund Utilization Report based on the actual status of the fund
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant did not submit the revised Fund Utilization Report (FUR). The only action taken by the management is the submission of FUR to DPWH.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) Include the cost of signboards in the project cost as provided under COA Circular No. 2013-004; b) Require the contractor to post signboard, tarpaulins or signboards as soon as the award has been made and must be suitably framed for outdoor display at the project location; and c) Submit to the Audit Team the required Quarterly Reports on Publicized Projects/Programs/Activities.
observations.managementAction: Cost of project signboard are included in the POW. Pictures of posted signboards were not attached in the DVs on the first partial billing. Some signboards were posted upon our ocular inspection.
observations.reason: Pictures of posted signboards were not attached to the DVs for the first partial billing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Administrator, Municipal Treasurer, Human Resource Management Officer and other concerned officials to study and consider the shifting to automated payroll system to tighten internal control over payroll funds and minimize operating costs.
observations.managementAction: Automated payroll system implemented in the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) Require management to submit justification on the procurement of mobile phones/tablets and motor vehicles
observations.managementAction: b) Strictly implement PPAs which are supportive of the objectives and mission of the Government
observations.reason: and d) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from approving OR for disbursements without an existing budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) The Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit POW, plans and drawings and technical specification with detailed breakdown of the cost up to completion of the project; and b) MPDO, Budget Officer, Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned to evaluate and review POW of the prioritized development projects to identify which projects or portion of projects will be implemented that can be readily useful to the constituents and no construction shall be started for portions of projects that are less than usable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and MPDO prepared POWs for projects. Some projects were constructed in phases due to budget constraints.
observations.reason: Some required documents were not submitted timely and several projects remained phased

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers as the basis in recording the RPT/SEF tax receivables at the beginning of the year and to set up the CY 2017 & 2018 RPT/SEF Receivables
observations.managementAction: and b) Municipal Accountant to use the Certified List of Taxpayers prepared by the Office of the City Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SEF tax receivables.
observations.reason: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Instruct personnel concerned to conduct regular reconciliation of records
observations.managementAction: b) Require the Property Custodian to prepare and submit SSMI
observations.reason: Supplies ledger cards and stock cards were not regularly maintained. No regular reconciliation of records was performed at time of audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) A list of the Municipality's programs/projects/activities be posted in signboards or bulletin boards following the provisions of the cited circular
observations.managementAction: b) Maintain updated Project Status based on monthly monitoring report prepared by Municipality
observations.reason: Some signboards were posted upon our ocular inspection. Updating of project status monthly was not regularly prepared. Required Quarterly Reports on Publicized Projects were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit lacking supporting documents and justify reasons for delays and liquidated damages
observations.managementAction: furnish COA with delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance
observations.reason: Some projects were completed but delivery documents were not furnished within 24 hours as required. Some settlements of liquidated damages and adjustments were being processed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers, payrolls and liquidation reports with supporting documents, cancelled checks, a report on the actual status of checks and the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms.
observations.managementAction: Some DVs/payrolls/liquidation reports were submitted upon our demand. Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) was not consistently submitted.
observations.reason: Partial submission only

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management refund the amount paid for the representation expenses and t-shirt allowance and to refrain from giving allowance and incentive without legal basis.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality refunded the amount received as identified representation expenses and t-shirt allowance. The LGU issued an official receipt in the total amount.
Despite our submission of OR for the refund of the disallowed amount no NSSDC has been issued yet to LGU and it still appeared in the prior year audit findings as unimplemented.
observations.reason: Partial settlement

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management refund the amount paid to AMOEC for the project and propose/implement a project which will serve its purpose.
observations.managementAction: Submitted justification and partial settlement. P46,535.60 of P775,750.00 was settled.
observations.reason: Partial settlement only

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following personnel the
following courses of action:
a) The Department Heads with unliquidated cash advance for PCF to return
the unused fund;
b) The Municipal Accountant to set up at the beginning of the year the PCF
under the imprest system and refrain from processing payment if the
supporting documents are inadequately accomplished or not attached;
c) The Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents for the
November replenishment of PCF; and
d) The Municipal Treasurer refrain from paying expenses that are not eligible
to be charged against the PCF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Inventory Committee to prepare a systematic and well-planned
physical inventory taking, which could be done in a year-round basis, until
all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality have been
accounted for, and to prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and ensure that all the
necessary data are provided thereon; and,
b) Direct the Property and Accounting Unit to undertake collaborative
procedure to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly taken
up in their respective records and that the Property Cards (PCs) maintained
by the Property Unit and PPELCs maintained by the Accounting Unit are
reconciled.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake the following courses of
action:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records per funded
project for all funds. Likewise, provide the necessary supporting schedules
for Due to NGAs and submit MOA of fund transfers with balances in the
books; and,
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to refund the
excess amounts of fund transfers from various source agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to properly
coordinate and reconcile the amounts reflected in the report prepared and
maintained by their corresponding offices;
b. The Municipal Accountant to properly monitor and maintain an accurate
subsidiary record of transactions and balances of the Trust Liabilities –
DRRM account;
c. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the current
year unexpended QRF and DRRMF- MOOE to the Special Trust Fund
under Trust Liabilities-DRRM account at year-end.
d. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF;
e. The LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of
DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that
only actual project expenses shall be reported in the utilization and
liquidation of Fund Transfer from any source agencies;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a revised Fund
Utilization Report based on the actual status of the fund; and
c. Make representation with the head of DPWH regarding the actual status of
the Fund Transfer to come up with an appropriate action in order to rectify
the deficiency in the utilization and liquidation of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC the following
courses of action:
a) To ensure that goods be procured based on relevant characteristics,
functionality and/or performance requirements and not on brand names;
and
b) To prepare BAC Resolution to duly authorize the delegation of authority to
the requesting unit/end-user.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to
the following courses of action:
a) Include the cost of signboards in the project cost awarded provided that such
signboard conforms to the specifications set forth under COA Circular No.
2013-004 dated January 30, 2013;
b) Require the contractor to post signboard, tarpaulins or similar materials as
soon as the award has been made and must be suitably framed for outdoor
display at the project location; and
c) Submit to the Audit Team the required Quarterly Reports on Publicized
Projects/Programs/Activities.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:
a) Establish internal control procedures on the processing of payroll, in
coordination with other concerned officials;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that paid payrolls be duly
supported with the required supporting documents and bear the approval of
the authorized signatory as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012; and
c) Direct the department office heads to prepare and submit the Daily Time
Records, Leave Applications, Travel Orders, Certificates of Appearances
and other pertinent documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Administrator, Municipal Treasurer, Human Resource Management Officer
and other concerned officials and employees to study and consider the shifting
to automated payroll system over manual system to tighten internal control over
payroll funds and minimize operating costs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake the following courses of
action:
a. Require the recipients of the herein covered procured mobile phones/tablets
and motor vehicles to submit justification on the necessity of such
procurement;
b. Strictly implement PPAs which are supportive of the objectives and mission
of the Municipal Government;
c. Direct the Bid and Awards Committee to procure assets in accordance with
the APP/SPP; and
d. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from approving Obligation
Request for disbursements without an existing budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following officials:
a. The Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit the POW, plans and
drawings and technical specification with detailed breakdown of the cost up
to completion of the project. POW for the phases should likewise be
submitted, so as to appropriately allocate funds and ensure continuity of
works; and
b. MPDO, Budget Officer, Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned to
evaluate and review POW of the prioritized development projects in order
to identify which projects or portion of projects will be implemented that can
be readily useful to the constituents and no construction shall be started for
portions of projects that are less than usable.

We recommended for the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of the
GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related program and projects
in order to attain the objective for which the funds were provided.

1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Stop providing depreciation allowance on assets that are already fully
depreciated and set-up as depreciation with due consideration on the
salvage value for each PPE; and
b. Prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the overstatement in
Accumulated Depreciation and Government Equity, and submit a copy to
the audit team for verification.

2.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to the following courses of action:
a. Make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the
affected accounts;
b. Provide supporting documents on the journal entry made on the
forfeiture of the retention money, otherwise, the said journal entry should
be reversed;
c. Establish the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable to correct the balance
of the Bail Bonds Payable account; and
d. Submit to the audit team the Journal Entry Vouchers covering the
foregoing adjusting/correcting entries, for verification.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to make adjusting entries to revert to the Due to BIR account the
amount of the cancelled check, and the amount representing penalties paid.

4.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method in accounting for
purchases of inventories pursuant to COA Circular No. 2002-003. We also
recommended that an SSMI should be prepared and submitted by the GSO
or Supply Officer as the basis of the Accountant in recording the inventory
as expense.

5.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) create an Inventory Committee in accordance with Section 156 of the
Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Local
Governments prescribed by COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20,
1992, and direct said committee to conduct properly the required physical
count of all PPEs of the LGU and prepare the required report thereon;
b) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Card; and
c) require the General Services Officer, if one is appointed, or the Supply
and Property Officer to maintain Property Cards.

6.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following officers:
a. Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete and proper supporting
documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers (DV), as stated in
Box B of the DV, and in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, submit
the lacking documents; and
b. Municipal Engineer to check and review the billing documents of the
contractor and ensure that it is supported with the appropriate SWA and
updated pictures.

7.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer
to the following courses of action:
a) Ensure that the cost of signboards was included in the project cost,
provided that such conform to the specifications set forth under COA
Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and
b) Require the contractor to post signboard, tarpaulins or similar materials
as soon as the award has been made and which should be suitably framed
for outdoor display at the project location.

8.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that remittances of withheld
taxes should be made promptly and intact to avoid the incurrence of penalties.

9.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget
Officer to the following courses of actions:
a.) Require all drivers of motor vehicles to prepare Trip Ticket for each
authorized travel, to properly supply the entries required in the Driver’s
Trip Tickets, and to prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels for each
motor vehicle; and
b.) Direct the personnel in-charge to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption for each motor vehicle.

10.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee Chairperson, in coordination with BAC Secretariats and
Municipal Accountant, to submit the lacking documentary requirements in
compliance to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and GPPB
Resolution No. 18-2020 dated September 16, 2020, respectively.

11.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to do the
following:
a) Submit the lacking documentary requirements as required in the DSWD
Memorandum Circular No. 4; and
b) Provide in a document the determination of vulnerable sector to which
the beneficiaries belong, to determine the documentary requirements
needed in the pay-out of like assistance, in the future, if any.

12.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
a. The Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to prepare
the required annual LDRRMF Investment Plan which would clearly
reflect the 4 thematic areas of NDRRMP; and
b. The Municipal Budget Officer to maintain the required registries for
LDRRMF, and make reconciliation of the balances in the LDRRMF
Utilization Report prepared by the Municipal Accountant.

13.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal person
to allocate at least 5% of LGUs annual budget to GAD-related PPAs fund,
and enjoin the GFPS to optimally utilize the GAD budget.

14.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the Local Finance
Committee the following:
a) Include in the Annual Budget an appropriation equivalent to 1% of IRA
to finance programs for Local Council for the Protection of Children;
and
b) Ensure that programs enumerated in the Annual Investment Plan is
consistent with the Annual Budget.

14.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development
Officer ensure the preparation of Work and Financial Plan annually so as to
prioritize programs according to the needs in the locality.

15.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget
Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to explain the
following:
a) the payment to Jhunarton Gasoline Station despite actual fuel
requisitions from AMOEC Gas Station; and
b) the discrepancy of total fuel expenses per sales invoices of Jhunarton
Gasoline Station and delivery receipts of AMOEC Gas Station.

16.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct both the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to stop the practice of charging
prior years expenses to the current year appropriations pursuant to the
pertinent provisions of RA 7160 and PD 1445. Otherwise, they shall be
personally liable for violation thereof.

17.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning
Development Coordinator and all other concerned to implement the
programs/projects which have not yet been started in order to ensure that
the 20% Development Fund is promptly and fully utilized pursuant to
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

17.14 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Budget Officer strictly monitor the expenditures and obligations incurred so
as not to exceed its appropriations.

18.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Agriculturist, Sangguniang Bayan, and other concerned officials to
immediately commence operations of the projects for the benefit of the
constituents and additional income for the LGU.

We reiterated our audit recommendation to:
a. Conduct the physical inventory of all PPEs. Prescribed composition
of the committee for the conduct of physical inventory is provided in
Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
While prescribed forms to be used in the reporting is provided in the
Manual for NGAS;
b. Reconcile the balances recorded in the Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) of the Accounting Office with the
balances reported in the Property Cards (PC) maintained by the
Property Officer/GSO;
c. Require all heads of the department to prepare a list of all existing
properties under their respective offices and submit the same to the
inventory committee to facilitate the inventory taking.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledger for each project funded by
various NGAs, indicating the separate and distinct purpose of each fund,
to facilitate the recording of transactions and reflect accurate excesses of
fund for return to source agencies; and
b. Regularly prepare and submit Fund Utilization Reports to the Audit
Team, review or identify the errors discussed, and draw JEV to effect the
necessary adjustment/s.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Make adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected
accounts to reconcile the book and bank balances;
b) Stop drawing checks for the payment of loan amortization since LBP has
been automatically debiting it to the LGUs IRA; and
c) Classify liabilities as to current and non-current and present accordingly
in the Statement of Financial Position of succeeding years.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to review the computation of the depreciation and observe the
established rules provided in paragraph 6 of IPSAS 17-Property, Plant and
Equipment (PPE). We also recommended that the necessary adjusting entry
be prepared to rectify the error committed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator to carefully plan the identification
of priority development projects and exert effort to fully implement the
identified developmental projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Immediate filling-up of the three (3) DRRM position to begin the
institutionalization of the organization of LDDRM Office for better
operations of LDRRM Office;
b. Accountant to prepare correcting entry on the recorded assistance
received from LBP; and
c. Budget Officer to stop charging disbursements not related to disaster
risk response management against the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate Registry of
Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation (RAAO) for LDRRM Fund;
b. Municipal Accountant to transfer the remaining unexpended LDRRM
Fund in CY 2018; present the details of LDRRM Fund using the
prescribed format shown below and revert back to Unappropriated
Surplus under General Fund the CY 2013 Unexpended LDRRM Fund
as it has lapsed a period of 5 years.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the:
MENRO Officer-in-charge to implement the different Solid Waste
Management PPAs as planned and budgeted every year;
a. Conduct seminars and training for the garbage collectors on proper
handling of solid wastes;
b. Procure Personal Protective Equipment for the garbage collectors; and
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to stop charging
Representation Allowances and other ineligible expenses to the fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Assessor to exert effort in implementing the CY 2012 General Revision, and
to take appropriate action in seeking for needed support to address
encountered difficulties in the course of implementation.

To correct the deficiencies, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the
HRMO:
a. To determine the necessity, assess the duties and functions, taking into
account their expertise, experience and capacity to handle the position in
order to come up with the ideal number of personnel to be hired;
b. To provide in the contract of service the specific details or information
such as expected service to be rendered, funding charges, daily rate and
to have it signed by the JO employee as acknowledgement to the
agreement;
c. Require each JO worker to accomplish their daily time records based on
actual check in and check out time using biometric machine or bundy
clock;
d. Prepare the accomplishment report of actual work done; and
e. Observe proper and complete documentation in the payment of wages.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned
officials or persons liable to settle the Notice of Suspensions and Notice of
Disallowances within the prescribed period.

We recommended further for the Municipal Accountant to send copy of the
Notices to Persons Liable for their information on their unsettled
suspensions/disallowance.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to include
all benefits and remuneration received by the Municipal officials and
employees in the computation of their respective taxable income, withhold
the correct amount of tax and remit the same on time to avoid exposing the
withholding agents of the LGU to the risk of incurring penalties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to remit the mandatory contributions to GSIS, PHIC and
HDMF as well as the loan amortizations of the LGU officials and employees
within the prescribed period as mandated by the laws and stop the payment
of fines, penalties and interest using public funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the heads of implementing departments/offices to ensure
implementation within the planned timeframe, the GAD PPAs which
address identified gender issues;
b. Instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and
Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from providing lump sum
appropriation in the GFAB and AIP to facilitate identification of PPAs
towards the GAD Plan and Budget, instead observe the provision in the
JMC if there are changes in the DILG-approved GPB and/or additional
PPAs to be implemented; and
c. Establish thru the GAD Focal Point System, a GAD Monitoring and
Evaluation Team as required in Sections 3.4 and 5.1 to 5.2 of PCW-DBM-
DILG-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, the following courses of action:
a. Refrain from approving for payment for reimbursements to Municipal
officials and employees, except for travels, emergency purchase, and
petty operating expenses; and
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to set up Petty Cash Fund and use the
same for petty operating expenses only.

We also recommended for the Bids and Awards Committee to use the
alternative methods of procurement provided under the Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184, if public bidding is not
feasible.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to strictly observe proper implementation of the guidelines set
forth in Budget Circular Nos. 2016-4 dated April 28, 2016 and 2017-4 dated
May 8, 2017 on the release of Mid-Year and Year-End Bonuses and stop the
early disbursement of such benefits.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer find ways and means to address the cash deficits the
municipality had been experiencing for several years now by exercising
diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government
funds.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare and submit a duly approved Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) and
all its pertinent supporting documents and schedule for all transactions in
the General Journal and Special Journals.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the adjustment for the
stale checks to reflect the correct balance of the cash accounts;
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit timely the
bank reconciliation statements with the bank statement and original
copies of paid checks as prescribed under Section 74 of PD 1445 and
COA Circular No. 96-011; and
3. Submit the list of deposits in transit and other items for adjustment
by the bank, indicated in the BRS, and the negotiated and cancelled
checks pursuant to the pertinent paragraph 3.0 and 3.5 of COA
Circular 96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Inventory Team, Office of the Municipal Accountant and
the Treasurer Office to conduct a proper inventory of the LGU’s PPEs
to determine physical existence and status of the PPEs, and submit the
report in accordance with the prescribed format and timeline;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to
maintain a complete and updated PPELC and PCs respectively for each
class of property and regularly reconcile their records and coordinate
with each other for the provision of depreciation and determination of
impairment losses of the assets.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to instruct the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to:









Prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names,
amount due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to
the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SEF tax
receivable in the succeeding year;
Ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the
addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other
follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and
other collection strategy that may be implemented by Management;
and
Introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the
tax collection of the city;
b) Municipal Accountant to use the Certified List of Taxpayers prepared by
the Office of the City Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SEF
tax receivables at the beginning of the year and to set up the CY 2017 &
2018 RPT/SET Receivables.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned
officials and employees to:
a) liquidate immediately their long outstanding cash advances and
compel them to submit liquidation papers and refund the unused cash
advances, if any;
b) monitor closely the liquidation of outstanding cash advances by
sending written reminder/demand on the submission of liquidation
report within the reglementary period; and
c) stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those who
have unliquidated balances.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare and submit General Journal and Special Journal in the
prescribed format as required by the NGAS Manual for LGU which
provide column for JEV, Sundry credit and Posting reference column.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO to submit a
record of the JO employees detailing the expected work to be performed
and/or output and require each payee to prepare and submit the
accomplishment report duly signed by the immediate supervisor. We further
recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the HRMO to prepare the
manning schedule, specifically, the laborers/cleaners and ensure that the daily
time record reflect the actual hours worked by an employee.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit the quarterly Report on SEF Utilization
in order to timely monitor the implementation and operations of SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MSWDO,
Municipal Accountant, BAC and other concerned to conform with the
implementing procedures and scheme provided by the DSWD and SIA in the
execution of the supplemental feeding program to ensure full attainment of
the program’s objective. Further, to submit the required documents for the
disbursement transaction to ascertain propriety and validity of the claims.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the:
a) MSWDO to cause the immediate submission of the required documents
as prescribed under the rules and procedures of the DSWD Guidelines to
guarantee attainment of the primary purpose of the social protection
program; and
b) Municipal Treasurer for the immediate liquidation of his cash advances
and submit complete supporting documents of his liquidation.

We recommended that LCE instruct MSWDO, Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary documents prescribed under
the rules and procedures of the DSWD Guidelines on the Expanded AICS to
prove the eligibility and entitlement of the listed client/beneficiaries of the
program. And the Municipal Treasurer to immediately liquidate her cash
advances and submit complete supporting documents of her liquidation.

We recommended that LCE admonish Project Development Officer to start
immediately the Skill Training Program and to submit a written
explanation/justification as to the cause of the delay.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the department heads of
the implementing offices of funds received from various national agencies for
the timely implementation and completion of projects and supervise and
monitor the completion of all remaining projects without further delay for the
enjoyment of benefits of the targeted beneficiaries therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned personnel
to:
a) Submit the MOA and monthly FUR on the sources and utilization of
fund to conduct monitoring and evaluation on the project
implementation and to ensure that all components and activities were
performed in line with the programmed design and is achieving its
objectives;
b) Prepare the overall status of physical and financial accomplishment
reports of SLP and SPP projects as basis in ascertaining the progress
and impact of the livelihood projects implementation and whether the
objectives of the fund were attained.

We recommended that LDRRMO under the direction of the Local Chief
Executive to prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the three (3) DRRM position be filled immediately to
begin the institutionalization of the organization of LDDRMO for better
operations and implementation of LDRRM.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned
department heads to optimally utilize the LDRRMF in order to uphold the
rights of the people to life and properties through preparing for disasters,
mitigating its effect, responding and recovering after disasters.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to remit the taxes withheld intact to lessen the liability
and accountability of the LGU, and prevent the possibility of using funds for
other purposes.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the schedules or list of some General Ledgers accounts and the corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers as well as the supporting documents for all the entries in the General Journal. Likewise, prepare completely filled up JEVs for all transactions, General Journal and special journals in the prescribed format which include column for JEVs and adjusting entries as well as rectify the other deficiencies noted. Henceforth, strictly follow the accounting rules and regulations.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still did not submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant strictly observe their respective responsibilities in handling the resources of the government. Likewise, we recommend that a review of the financial management and operations of the LGU be made to enable to pay the current obligations. These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance as a result of the post of the related transactions.
observations.reason: The management still failed to comply the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit justification for the excess of Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses as against the appropriations. Henceforth, strictly incur expenses within the approved appropriation/budget, otherwise the excess would be disallowed in audit. Likewise, we recommend that management submit the lacking disbursement vouchers, payrolls and supporting documents and rectify the deficiencies noted. Henceforth, refrain from payment and undergoing procurement process without complete documentations. Further, we recommend that management adhere to the provision that repairs should not exceed 30% of the current market price of same or similar equipmentas required in COA Circular No. 2012-003 as well as evaluate the condition of the motor vehicle before a repair be undertaken to be cost effective.Lastly, the Municipal Accountant shall maintain a record of the repairs for each government equipment indicating therein the type of repair undertaken, the date when it was made, and other pertinent data as required in COA Circular No. 81-131A.We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries of the erroneously recorded transaction.
observations.reason: The management still failed to comply the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit the lacking supporting documents and justification for the special hire as well as rectify the deficiencies noted. Likewise, we recommend that management refrain from having transactions with suppliers where pecuniary interest exists, require the contractors/suppliers to issue official receipt in gross amount and implement its projects within the specified contract time, plus any time extension granted and if warranted, collect the necessary liquidated damages from the non-compliant contractors. Henceforth, refrain from payment and undergoing procurement process without complete documentations and ensure the authenticity of the documents submitted by the bidders during bidding process.
Further, we recommend that the BAC submit justification why a legal consultant was hired without the proper procurement process; why liquidated damages were not collected despite delays in the completion of the projects; why the Repair/Improvement of RHU Lying-In was done in twenty-one days (21) which was thirty-nine (39) days earlier than the sixty (60) days project duration per contract; and why COA was not informed of the deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance.
Lastly, we recommend that official responsible for accepting deliveries of procured items furnish COA with delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after such acceptance.
observations.reason: The management still failed to comply the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management submit justifications of the fund transfers from DSWD fund and common trust fund to BUB fund totaling P2,569,762.50, the erroneous deposit of receipt from DSWD RO 8 to the General Fund amounting to P877,500.00 and why undergo the procurement process of the project Construction of Wharf and Artificial Coral Reef Sanctuary costingP3,295,430.28 despite unavailability of funds. Likewise, submit the lacking supporting documents and rectify the other deficiencies noted. Further, we recommend that projects be immediately implemented upon receipt of the funds for the benefit of the constituents/beneficiaries.
These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance upon verification of the documents to be submitted.
observations.reason: The management still failed to comply the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the LDRRMO prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP and the required monthly reports in the prescribed format and period; the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the prescribed recording and reporting guidelines; and the management transfer the 30% QRF totaling P998,415.35 to the Trust Fund, rectify the deficiencies noted and submit the lacking supporting documents. 
Henceforth, strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-002, COA Circular No. 2014-002 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 with regards to the LDRRMF.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Notice of Disallowance as a result of the post audit of the required documents.
observations.reason: The management still failed to comply the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management inform the bank not to deduct from the SEF bank account any loan payments, submit the deposit slip for the replenishment of the amount deducted and the lacking supporting documents, refrain from granting financial assistance, furnish the audit team a copy of the approved Annual School Board Plan and Budget for CY 2016 and rectify the other deficiencies noted. Likewise, utilize the fund to those expenses specified by law. Henceforth, strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 100(a) and (c) and 272 of RA 7160 and Section 1 of RA 5447 with regards to the utilization of the Special Education Funds.
observations.reason: The management still failed to comply the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board actively perform the functions required by law and prepare the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan covering the period CY 2016-2025. Likewise, we recommend that management provide a separate appropriation for the projects/programs/activities under the solid waste management program. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.reason: The management still failed to comply the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the concerned officials and employees submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers, payrolls and liquidation reports with supporting documents, the cancelled checks, a report on the actual status of checks noted as gaps in the series of checks issued and the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms.  Henceforth, management should adhere strictly with the pertinent provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.  
These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance as a result of non-compliance and/or audit of the documents to be submitted.
observations.reason: The management still failed to comply the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned LGU and Job Order Personnel to refund the amount received as representation expenses and t-shirt allowance. Henceforth, refrain from giving allowance and incentive without legal basis. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.reason: The management still failed to comply the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management refund the amount paid to AMOEC for the project “Tricycle Driver’s Spare Parts and Services Trading” and “Padyak Servicing and Spare Parts Trading” totaling P775,750.00 and submit justification why the “Wharf I” project was not put into use. Henceforth, propose/implement project which will serve its purpose, will be used upon completion and are much needed for the development of the community to avoid loss or wastage of government funds.
observations.reason: The management still failed to comply the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory team that would conduct the periodic physical count of property, plant and equipment, prepare the corresponding report, and submit to the Auditor concerned within the deadline set. Likewise, the Accounting and Property Unit of the LGU should maintain property records to facilitate the tracing and verification of account balances. Furthermore, the agency should submit list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) during the year and those that are still to be implemented and inform the audit team that appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, to assist in the verification of the Construction in Progress Account.
observations.reason: Management did not conduct an inventory of its PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and other accountable/concerned officers to immediately submit the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents for audit. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned officials and employees to:
Liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances by compelling them to submit liquidation papers and refund the unused cash advances, if any;
Monitor closely the liquidation of outstanding cash advances by sending written reminder/demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; and
Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated balances.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel concerned to:
Prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) regularly and submit to the Municipal Accountant as basis in preparing the journal entry voucher (JEV) to booked up the issuance of supplies and materials on stock.
Make a regular updating of the supplies ledger card/stock cards/Bin Cards; advise Accounting and the Supply Sections to conduct regular reconciliation of their records.
Conduct physical count/inventory of said items every semester as prescribed in the National Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual.
Prepare Subsidiary Ledgers as prescribed by Section 111 (1) of PD 1445.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance and/or Notice of Suspension as a result of post-audit.
During the exit conference, the management acknowledged the observations and promised to comply with the recommendations.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
Give priority for the creation of an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE.
Direct the Head of the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to hasten the updating of their records to facilitate the reconciliation of accounting and property records.
Oblige the Municipal Accountant to review the details of properties which are non-functional or unserviceable and prepare the necessary adjustments, if warranted.
Require the committee to prepare a methodical and well planned physical inventory taking, which could be done in a year round basis, until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality have been accounted for and listed in the RPCPPE. Also all AREs must be renewed regularly pursuant to existing rules and regulations.
Thereafter, the RPCPPE must be reconciled with the property record of each class of property at the General Services Office and subsidiary ledgers maintained at the Accounting Office. Any discrepancy should be investigated and appropriate action should be taken thereof.
During the exit conference, the management agreed with the observations and promised to comply with the recommendations.
We recommend strict compliance with the accounting and reporting guidelines for local road network construction and maintenance system prescribed by .COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommend that the Agency submit justification for the excess of Personal Services and Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses as against the Appropriation. Admonish the Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly incur expenses within the approved Budget/Appropriation.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance as a result of the evaluation of the justification and the submitted documents.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant:
Ensure that only expenditures/projects enumerated under the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 are considered in the charging against the 20% Development Fund and
Strictly utilize the fund in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the JMC to fully achieve the intention and purpose for which it was established.
Restore to the 20% Development Fund the ineligible expenditures that were improperly charged out of the same fund amounting to P4,526,438.06.
Ensure the full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the purpose/projects enumerated under DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommend that LGU strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 with regard to appropriation and utilization of the 5% of the revenue from the regular sources for disaster preparedness and mitigation to help achieve desirable protection and preservation of the lives and properties of the constituents of the municipality.
